vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-699: Conversation view renders tool calls out of terminal order

plan.title: Conversation view renders tool calls out of terminal order
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-7

narratives.Problem: parseConversationMessages emits entries in JSONL read order and never sorts. Parallel tool calls, buffered writes, and compact boundaries render out of terminal order. tool_result arriving before tool_use is orphaned. pendingToolUse map is rebuilt each incremental parse call, so cross-batch tool pairing is also broken.
narratives.Proposal: Capture a monotonic sequence per JSONL line, keep an unresolvedResults map for backward pairing, persist parse state across incremental calls, and sort messages + workLog by (createdAt, sequence) before returning. Propagate sequence through the wire contract so the client can sort defensively.

items.title: Add optional sequence field to ChatMessage and WorkLogEntry contracts

narrative.Action: In packages/contracts/src/rpc.ts, add `sequence: Schema.optional(Schema.Number)` to ChatMessage and WorkLogEntry. Rebuild contracts (cd packages/contracts && npm run build).

subItems.title: ChatMessage schema exposes optional numeric `sequence` field
WorkLogEntry schema exposes optional numeric `sequence` field
packages/contracts builds cleanly; `npm run typecheck` passes at repo root

items.title: Emit monotonic sequence on every parsed message and workLog entry

narrative.Action: In conversation-service.ts `parseConversationMessages`, maintain a local `sequence` counter that increments per JSONL line processed. Stash it on every ChatMessage and WorkLogEntry produced (user, assistant, pendingAssistant flush, tool_use, tool_result-resolved workLog push).

subItems.title: Every ChatMessage returned by parseConversationMessages has a numeric sequence
Every WorkLogEntry returned by parseConversationMessages has a numeric sequence matching the JSONL line index of its tool_use
sequence values are strictly monotonically increasing in emission order within a single parse call

items.title: Two-pass pairing: handle tool_result that arrives before tool_use

narrative.Action: Add an `unresolvedResults: Map<tool_use_id, {result, isError, sequence, createdAt}>` alongside `pendingToolUse`. On tool_result: if no matching pendingToolUse, stash in unresolvedResults (never drop). On tool_use: check unresolvedResults first and merge into a completed WorkLogEntry immediately if present. Never orphan a tool_result.

subItems.title: tool_result preceding its tool_use in the JSONL is paired correctly into workLog
No tool_result is silently dropped — all tool_result entries either pair with a tool_use or remain in unresolvedResults carried state
Existing forward-paired case (tool_use then tool_result) still works identically

items.title: Sort messages and workLog by (createdAt, sequence) before returning

narrative.Action: After the JSONL walk in parseConversationMessages, sort `messages` and `workLog` independently by (createdAt ISO string compare, then sequence). Return sorted arrays. Preserve streaming/totalCost/byteOffset fields unchanged.

subItems.title: Returned messages array is sorted by (createdAt, sequence)
Returned workLog array is sorted by (createdAt, sequence)
Identical timestamps are deterministically ordered by sequence

items.title: Persist pendingToolUse, unresolvedResults, and sequence across incremental parse calls

narrative.Action: Extend ParseResult with `parseState: { pendingToolUse, unresolvedResults, lastSequence }`. parseConversationMessages accepts an optional prior parseState as a second/third argument (alongside byteOffset) and seeds its maps + counter from it. Update the watchConversation caller in conversation-service.ts and any agents.ts callers to thread parseState through successive calls. Flushing of unpaired pendingToolUse only happens at boundaries that own the full window (e.g. summarizeConversationActivity, parseFromLastCompactBoundary non-incremental use), never mid-stream in the watcher.

subItems.title: A tool_use in parse call N pairs correctly with its tool_result in parse call N+1
sequence counter continues monotonically across incremental calls (does not reset to 0)
watchConversation and agents.ts conversation route thread parseState through successive reads
Unpaired tool_use entries are only flushed by non-incremental entry points, not by the watcher

items.title: Thread sequence through the agents.ts conversation response

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/server/routes/agents.ts, ensure the conversation response shape passes each message/workLog entry's `sequence` through untouched. Update any intermediate mapping that would drop unknown fields.

subItems.title: GET /api/agents/:id/conversation returns `sequence` on every message and workLog entry
No backwards-compatibility break: clients that ignore sequence still render correctly

items.title: MessagesTimeline defensive client-side sort by (createdAt, sequence)

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/chat/MessagesTimeline.tsx, apply a `.toSorted((a,b) => a.createdAt.localeCompare(b.createdAt) || (a.sequence ?? 0) - (b.sequence ?? 0))` to the combined timeline before render. Server is authoritative; this is a belt-and-braces tiebreaker.

subItems.title: Rendered timeline is sorted by (createdAt, sequence) regardless of server array order
Sort is stable and does not re-run on every render unless inputs change (useMemo or equivalent)

items.title: Unit tests for conversation-service ordering and pairing

narrative.Action: Create src/dashboard/server/services/conversation-service.test.ts with synthetic JSONL fixtures covering: (a) parallel tool calls in one assistant turn, (b) tool_result before its tool_use, (c) incremental parse boundary splitting a tool_use/tool_result pair, (d) three entries with identical timestamps using sequence as tiebreaker, (e) compact boundary mid-turn.

subItems.title: Parallel tool call fixture: messages + workLog sorted in terminal order, both tools paired
Backward-pairing fixture: tool_result preceding tool_use is correctly merged into workLog
Incremental fixture: two parseConversationMessages calls with state threading produce the same output as one full parse
Timestamp-tie fixture: three entries at identical timestamp are ordered by sequence
Compact boundary fixture: parseFromLastCompactBoundary returns correctly ordered post-boundary content
All new tests pass under `npm test`

items.title: Regression fixture from a real mis-ordered session

narrative.Action: Capture (or scrub and commit) a real ~/.claude JSONL session that currently renders out of order. Store under src/dashboard/server/services/__fixtures__/pan-699-regression.jsonl with any user/secret content redacted. Add a snapshot test asserting the expected ordered output.

subItems.title: Fixture JSONL exists in __fixtures__/ and is scrubbed of any secrets / PII
Snapshot test asserts ordered messages + workLog and fails if ordering regresses

items.title: Playwright UAT: conversation view renders in terminal order

narrative.Action: Add a Playwright spec under the existing dashboard UAT harness that opens an agent with a known-good session fixture and asserts the rendered conversation matches expected order (including parallel tool calls). Must use an isolated browser instance/profile — no shared state with other agents' sessions. All required login/setup reproducible inside the isolated session.

subItems.title: Playwright spec runs against an isolated browser instance/profile
Spec opens the dashboard conversation view and asserts rendered order matches expected sequence for a parallel-tool-call session
Spec does not depend on any other agent's Playwright browser or shared login state
Spec passes locally via the project's dashboard UAT runner
